### Fix 2
Line: 25
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: StackItem


### Fix 3

Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 30): `Button.variant="link"` type changed
The `variant` prop type added `'stateful'` to the union. The current value `"link"` is still valid in the new type. **No code change needed** ã `"link"` remains a valid value.

### Fix 2 (Line 25): Icon children to prop on Button
The icon (`ExternalLinkSquareAltIcon`) is already passed via the `icon` prop (line 26: `icon={<ExternalLinkSquareAltIcon />}`). The Button has text children (an `<a>` tag with "Learn more"). This is **already migrated** ã the icon is in the `icon` prop, not as children.

### Fix 3 (Lines 14, 21): StackItem new `component` prop
The `component` prop on `StackItem` accepts `React.ReactNode`. Looking at the usage:
- Line 14: `<StackItem>` wraps a `<Text>` (which will become `<Content>`) ã these are just children, not something that should be moved to `component`. The `component` prop on StackItem is for specifying the HTML element type (like `div`, `span`), not for absorbing children.
- Line 21: `<StackItem className="text-muted">` wraps text content and a Button.

This is an LLM-assisted rule suggesting we *review* whether content should move to `component`. In PatternFly, `StackItem`'s `component` prop specifies the rendered HTML element (similar to `component` on other PF components). The current children usage is correct. **No change needed.**

### Fix 4 (Line 5): `TextVariants` ã `ContentVariants`
Replace `TextVariants` with `ContentVariants` in the import and all usages. Line 15 uses `TextVariants.p` which maps to `ContentVariants.p` (member `p -> p`).

### Fix 5 (Line 5): `Text` ã `Content`
Replace `<Text>` with `<Content>` in JSX and import. Props map directly (component, className, children all stay the same).

Let me apply fixes 4 and 5 together:

1. Import: Change `Text, TextVariants` to `Content, ContentVariants`
2. Line 15: `<Text className="kv-sysprep-info" component={TextVariants.p}>` ã `<Content className="kv-sysprep-info" component={ContentVariants.p}>`
3. Line 19: `</Text>` ã `</Content>`

arguments.after: import { Button, Content, ContentVariants, Stack, StackItem } from '@patternfly/react-core';
arguments.before: import { Button, Stack, StackItem, Text, TextVariants } from '@patternfly/react-core';

<Content className="kv-sysprep-info" component={ContentVariants.p}>
            {t(
              'Sysprep is an automation tool for Windows that automates Windows setup, and custom software provisioning.',
            )}
          </Content>
arguments.before:
<Text className="kv-sysprep-info" component={TextVariants.p}>
            {t(
              'Sysprep is an automation tool for Windows that automates Windows setup, and custom software provisioning.',
            )}
          </Text>

content.text: Now let me verify the final file:

The file looks correct. All imports are clean, all references are updated, and the structure is preserved.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `"link"` remains valid in the new type union. The only change was adding `'stateful'` as a new option ã no existing values were removed. |
| **Fix 2** | Icon children to `icon` prop on Button | **Already applied** | The icon `<ExternalLinkSquareAltIcon />` was already passed via the `icon` prop (line 26), not as children. No change needed. |
| **Fix 3** | StackItem new `component` prop | **No change needed** | The `component` prop on `StackItem` specifies the HTML element to render (e.g., `div`, `span`), not content to absorb from children. The current children usage is correct and idiomatic. |
| **Fix 4** | `TextVariants` ã `ContentVariants` | **Applied** | Replaced `TextVariants` with `ContentVariants` in the import (line 5) and in JSX usage `TextVariants.p` ã `ContentVariants.p` (line 15). The member mapping `p -> p` confirms a direct rename. |
| **Fix 5** | `Text` ã `Content` | **Applied** | Replaced `Text` with `Content` in the import (line 5) and in JSX tags `<Text>` ã `<Content>` / `</Text>` ã `</Content>` (lines 15, 19). All props (`className`, `component`, `children`) map directly. |
